mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve has been raising FEDFUNDS steadily from its 2004 trough; when FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5% during an active tightening cycle, rising short-term discount rates compress equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and rising for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.63% in April 2005 reflecting ongoing Fed tightening from 1.0% trough in 2004
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following FEDFUNDS threshold crossings are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS signals expanding labor income and corporate revenue prospects, which continuously supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon regardless of level or regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132926 thousand persons in March 2005, trending upward
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 20-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated only while financing costs stay low; when FEDFUNDS rises while HOUST is simultaneously declining, this joint deterioration signals an impending contraction in residential investment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. Neither series alone captures this turning-point risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3 months AND HOUST declines > 5% over same window
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2195 thousand units in Feb 2005 amid rising FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-decline/FEDFUNDS-rise triggers fail to produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month expansion in INDPRO reflects strengthening industrial output and corporate earnings momentum, generating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in improving fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.527 in March 2005 continuing upward trend
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive association with trailing INDPRO growth (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates past a threshold associated with Fed concern (implying further rate hikes), markets reprice future discount rates upward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as tightening expectations increase.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 192.0 index level in Feb 2005 with inflation running near 3% YoY
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following CPI threshold breaches are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of already-low unemployment near cyclical troughs, a sustained rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market turning points historically associated with slowing consumption and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; this effect is conditional on the labor market being in a late-cycle low-unemployment regime rather than an early recovery regime.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months while UNRATE level < 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% in March 2005, near cyclical lows
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE upticks in low-unemployment regimes are not followed by negative 60-day forward returns in over 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, which continuously feeds positive short-term revisions to corporate revenue expectations, generating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 339299 million dollars in March 2005 continuing an upward trend
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with trailing RSAFS growth (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising alongside strong INDPRO growth and elevated CPIAUCSL inflation, the joint signal of an overheating economy under active tightening indicates markets are approaching a late-cycle peak, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the combination heightens recession and multiple-compression risk beyond what any single series predicts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising 3+ consecutive months AND INDPRO YoY growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:April 2005 snapshot shows FEDFUNDS at 2.63% rising, INDPRO at 118.527 growing, CPIAUCSL near 192.0 with inflation firming
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to produce significantly negative 20-day forward returns (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.
